response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, testing-focused engineer with genuine IDE-internals exposure (C/C++ work on NI LabWindows/CVI, a C IDE) and strong tooling habits, but the core requirements for this role are largely unmet. The JD demands strong Java and Kotlin with deep JVM understanding and solid Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate experience; the resume shows no Kotlin, no Spring or JVM enterprise frameworks, and only incidental Java use (robotic arms, a mobile orchestration tool). The bulk of experience is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. Despite relevant strengths in test-driven development and developer tooling, the JVM/enterprise-framework gap is too large for a JVM-tooling specialist position.
strengths: Direct IDE-internals experience building C/C++ components for the LabWindows/CVI IDE
Deep TDD, CI/CD, and developer-tooling craftsmanship across many roles
Broad systems range including low-latency, high-throughput services
concerns: No Kotlin and no Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate experience as required
Java exposure is incidental, with no evidence of deep JVM expertise
Primary stack (TypeScript/Node, DLang) is misaligned with JVM tooling focus

key_factors.factor: Missing Kotlin and Spring/JVM enterprise experience
Shallow Java depth relative to a JVM-tooling role
